2605.06342.title: Don't Lose Focus: Activation Steering via Key-Orthogonal Projections
2605.06342.paper_summary: Activation steering controls LLM behaviour towards target behaviour by intervening in internal representations, yet it often degrades reasoning and retrieval performance. We argue that a primary cause of this trade-off is attention rerouting: steering vectors alter query-key matching, shifting attention away from contextually important tokens toward less informative ones. To address this, we propose Steering via Key-Orthogonal Projections (SKOP), a steering method that constrains harmful attention rerouting without eliminating steering efficacy. SKOP achieves this by preserving attention patterns on a small set of focus tokens the model relies on for reasoning and retrieval, while allowing redistribution among less critical tail tokens. Across multiple steering benchmarks, we show that SKOP achieves the best joint steering-utility trade-off, reducing utility degradation by 5-7x while retaining over 95% of vanilla steering efficacy. Our results further suggest that, in long-context retrieval settings where vanilla steering approaches are ineffective, SKOP can maintain robust performance by avoiding attention rerouting.
2605.06342.paper_summary_zh: 啟動控制會通過干預內部表徵來引導 LLM 行為朝向目標行為，但它往往會降低推理和檢索性能。我們認為這種權衡的主要原因是注意力重新路由：引導向量改變查詢-鍵匹配，將注意力從上下文中重要的標記轉移到信息量較少的標記上。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了通過鍵正交投影的引導方法（SKOP），這是一種在不消除引導效果的情況下限制有害注意力重新路由的引導方法。SKOP 通過保留模型在推理和檢索中依賴的一小組焦點標記的注意力模式，同時允許在不那麼關鍵的尾部標記之間重新分配來實現這一點。在多個引導基準測試中，我們顯示 SKOP 實現了最佳的引導-效用權衡，將效用降級降低了 5-7 倍，同時保留了超過 95% 的原始引導效果。我們的結果進一步表明，在原始引導方法無效的長上下文檢索設置中，SKOP 可以通過避免注意力重新路由來保持穩健的性能。
2605.06342.author: Haoyan Luo et.al.
2605.06342.authors: Haoyan Luo, Mateo Espinosa Zarlenga, Mateja Jamnik
